Hennessy Industries, Llc?S Notice Of Discovery Motion And Motion For A Protective Order To Prohibit Duplicative Testimony From Hennessy Industries, Llc?S Corporate Representative; And Declaration Of Robert C. Rodriguez
Off Calendar Jun-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert C. Rodriguez of GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (619-230-7436), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Hennessy Industries, LLC.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff Regina Jo Miller, as Successor-In-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Joseph Miller, Deceased; Joann Hester, Susan Bender, and Wendy Miller, as Wrongful Death Heir of Joseph Miller, Deceased.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling as amended: Defendant Hennessy Industries, LLC's Motion for Protective Order to Prohibit Duplicative Testimony from Hennessy Industries, LLC's Corporate Representative is DENIED. Defendant failed to show that the burden, expense, or intrusiveness of that discovery clearly outweighs the likelihood that the information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.020(a); see also CCP 2025.420(b).) Plaintiffs continued the PMK deposition multiple times at Defendant's request so that the PMK could prepare to testify on behalf of Defendant as to all topics in Plaintiffs' original notice of deposition. (Renken Declaration at Exs. A-B, D.) The Court strikes paragraph 7 of the Rodriguez declaration as Defendant cannot quote statements made in the IDC in any subsequent formal Discovery Motion.

The Court strikes paragraph 4, page 4 of the Defendant's Reply, lines 18-21 and page 7, paragraph 4, line 15-22 of the Rodriguez declaration as Defendant cannot quote statements made in the IDC in the Reply. (Local Rule 20.1(C).)

Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant has not acted with substantial justification nor are there other circumstances that make the imposition of sanctions unjust. (CCP 2025.420(h).) Defendant is ordered to pay $3150.00 to Plaintiffs within 30 days of the date of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham of Veritext, CSR 13293 (415-274-9977) calendar-la@veritext.com (503/CML)
